name: Product Inventory Value Calculator
description: Create a CSV file with 10 sample products, read it back, and calculate total inventory value

tasks.name: Create Product CSV File
tasks.description: You MUST call file_write to create a CSV file named 'products.csv' with exactly 10 sample products. The CSV must have a header row (name,price,quantity) followed by 10 data rows. Use realistic product names (e.g., Laptop, Mouse, Keyboard), prices as decimal numbers (e.g., 999.99), and quantities as integers (e.g., 5). Example row: 'Laptop,999.99,5'. Output the full path of the created file.
tasks.success_criteria: Output contains the file path 'products.csv' and confirms the file was created with 10 products

tasks.name: Read Product CSV File
tasks.description: You MUST call file_read to read the 'products.csv' file created in task_001. Output the file contents verbatim. Do NOT summarize as 'file was read' — the next task needs the actual CSV data to perform calculations. Include all rows including the header.
tasks.success_criteria: Output contains the complete CSV text with header row 'name,price,quantity' and 10 data rows visible

tasks.name: Calculate Total Inventory Value
tasks.description: You MUST call python_exec to calculate the total inventory value from the CSV data in task_002's output. Write Python code that: 1) Parses the CSV text from task_002 (skip header row), 2) For each product, calculates value = price * quantity, 3) Sums all values, 4) Prints the total. Use the csv module or simple string parsing. Output format: 'Total Inventory Value: $XXXX.XX' with the final sum clearly stated.
tasks.success_criteria: Output contains 'Total Inventory Value: $' followed by a numeric value calculated from all 10 products

metadata.purpose: Demonstrate CSV file creation, reading, and computation workflow
